title: TURKESTERONE
description: Turkesterone is a phytoecdysteroid, a plant-derived compound structurally similar to insect molting hormones, found in plants like Ajuga turkestanica. It is claimed to enhance muscle protein synthesis through estrogen receptor binding and potential IGF-1 modulation, though human evidence remains limited. Marketed as a natural anabolic agent for muscle growth and athletic performance without the side effects of traditional steroids.

research.title: Ecdysteroids as non-conventional anabolic agent: performance enhancement by ecdysterone supplementation in humans
research.summary: A 10-week study in 46 young men found significantly higher increases in muscle mass and one-repetition bench press performance with ecdysterone supplementation compared to placebo, with no liver or kidney toxicity observed.

research.title: Impact of Acute Turkesterone Dosing on Serum IGF-1 and Gastrointestinal Tolerance
research.summary: Study of 11 recreationally active males found no significant effects on serum IGF-1 levels at doses up to 2000mg, and no participants reported gastrointestinal distress symptoms.

research.title: Ecdysterone and Turkesterone—Compounds with Prominent Potential in Sport and Healthy Nutrition
research.summary: Review examining naturally occurring ecdysterone and turkesterone in plants, discussing their potential advantages in sports nutrition and healthy dietary applications.

sentiment.summary: User consensus is predominantly skeptical, with most considering turkesterone ineffective or a scam. Independent lab testing revealed most products contain little to no actual turkesterone, and users report minimal to no noticeable benefits despite high costs.

effects.label: Product Quality Issues:
effects.description: Independent lab testing shows most commercial turkesterone products contain minimal to no actual active ingredient, with widespread mislabeling

effects.label: Limited Human Evidence:
effects.description: Despite animal studies showing promise, human research is sparse and results on IGF-1 and anabolic effects are inconclusive or negative

effects.label: Comparison to Alternatives:
effects.description: Users consistently note that basic compounds like creatine provide far more reliable and cost-effective results

effects.label: Scientific Skepticism:
effects.description: Even when products are legitimate, the compound appears to have minimal practical effect on muscle building or performance in humans

dosage.label: Common Dosing:
dosage.description: Users typically try 500-1000mg daily, with some going up to 2000mg split into two doses

dosage.label: Cycling Approach:
dosage.description: Some users cycle turkesterone with periods on and off, though no clear evidence supports this practice

dosage.label: Duration Trials:
dosage.description: Most users test for 2-3 months before concluding it's ineffective, with few continuing long-term use

dosage.label: Timing Irrelevant:
dosage.description: No consensus on optimal timing, as most users see no effects regardless of when taken

sideEffects.label: Minimal Side Effects:
sideEffects.description: Users report virtually no negative side effects, which is consistent with it likely containing little active ingredient

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Tolerance:
sideEffects.description: Research confirms good GI tolerance even at high doses of 2000mg with no reported distress

sideEffects.label: Hormonal Safety:
sideEffects.description: No reports of hormonal disruption, gynecomastia, or testosterone suppression unlike actual anabolic compounds

sideEffects.label: Financial Cost:
sideEffects.description: The main 'side effect' reported is wasted money on an expensive and ineffective product

availability.label: Widespread Scamming:
availability.description: The supplement market is flooded with fake or under-dosed turkesterone products, with legitimate sourcing extremely difficult

availability.label: High Cost:
availability.description: Products are expensive ($40-80+ per bottle) relative to their ineffectiveness and questionable authenticity

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Fully legal and widely available online and in supplement stores, though WADA has considered banning ecdysteroids in competitive sports

availability.label: Source Authenticity:
availability.description: Historical claims about Uzbekistan being the only legitimate source, with ongoing debates about which brands, if any, contain real turkesterone
